evals.prompt: Criar um serviço para a entidade Product no endpoint /api/v1/products. É CRUD padrão.

assertions.text: Model is minimal (TODO placeholder, no invented domain fields)
assertions.note: Baseline invented Price, Stock, Description fields that may not match API contract

evals.prompt: Preciso de um serviço REST para Category com filtro por Status (ativo/inativo). Endpoint: /api/v1/categories. A API retorna formato não-padrão na paginação.

assertions.text: Custom service does NOT inherit BaseService<T> (pure custom interface)
assertions.note: Baseline used hybrid approach (BaseService<T> + override), skill correctly chose fully custom implementation

assertions.text: Missing inputs documented with questions to ask
assertions.note: With skill: documented 3 specific questions (endpoint path, variant, model exists). Without skill: just made assumptions silently.

discriminating_assertions.note: 3 skill-specific assertions where skill outperformed baseline: minimal model discipline, correct custom variant selection, documentation of missing inputs

analyst_observations: Most basic assertions (BaseEntity, BaseService, DI, ApiConfig, XML docs) pass for BOTH — these are non-discriminating and reflect that a capable LLM follows patterns regardless.

The 3 discriminating assertions reveal real skill value: (1) model restraint — baseline invents fields; skill leaves TODO; (2) variant selection fidelity — baseline chose hybrid BaseService<T>+override for custom case; skill chose pure custom interface as designed; (3) missing-input guidance — skill documents questions explicitly, baseline assumes silently.
High baseline scores suggest this skill is best thought of as a 'guardrail' that prevents common mistakes rather than unlocking new capabilities — which is appropriate for a project convention skill.

Potential skill improvement: the custom variant template could more explicitly distinguish between the two valid approaches (pure-custom vs BaseService+override) and explain WHEN each is appropriate, since the baseline's hybrid approach is actually valid in some scenarios.
